tools.name: osop.init
tools.description: Initialize OSOP in a project directory: creates sessions/ and adds an OSOP section to CLAUDE.md. Idempotent — safe to run on a project that's already initialized.

project_dir.description: Absolute path to the project root. Defaults to current working directory.

tools.name: osop.validate
tools.description: Validate an OSOP workflow (.osop) or execution log (.osoplog) against the canonical schema. Returns errors with field locations. Use before record/replay.

content.description: The OSOP YAML content to validate.

file_path.description: Path to an .osop.yaml or .osoplog.yaml file to validate.

schema_variant.description: Schema variant: 'core' (4 node types) or 'full' (all types).

tools.name: osop.record
tools.description: Execute an OSOP workflow via the full executor (cost limits, risk_assess pre-flight, agent/api/sub-agent dispatch) and produce an .osoplog at the end. Use osop.replay for streaming/durable execution of cli+human workflows.

content.description: The OSOP YAML content to execute.

file_path.description: Path to an .osop.yaml file to execute.

allow_exec.description: Allow CLI nodes to execute shell commands.

tools.name: osop.replay
tools.description: Execute an .osop workflow with live .osoplog streaming via the LiveLog SDK — partial state stays durable on crash. v1 supports cli + human nodes; agent/api are recorded as SKIPPED with a TODO marker. For full executor use osop.record.

file_path.description: Path to an .osop.yaml file to execute.

allow_exec.description: Allow cli nodes to actually run shell commands.

interactive.description: Pause for input on human nodes.

continue_on_error.description: Don't halt on FAILED node; mark and continue.

output_dir.description: Directory where the .osoplog is written (default: cwd).

timeout_seconds.description: Default per-node timeout in seconds (overridable via node.timeout_sec).

tools.name: osop.log
tools.description: Synthesize an .osop workflow definition + .osoplog execution record from a Claude Code transcript JSONL — uses real tool-call evidence, not LLM self-report. Source can be a session id (looked up under ~/.claude/projects/), an explicit jsonl path, or omitted (auto-resolves the most recent transcript for the project_dir).

source.description: Session id, transcript .jsonl path, or omitted for auto-resolve.

project_dir.description: Project directory used for auto-resolve when source is omitted.

short_desc.description: Short description for output filenames (default: derived from date+session id).

tags.description: Extra tag(s) attached to the .osop output.

tools.name: osop.diff
tools.description: Compare two .osop workflows or two .osoplog execution logs structurally. Returns added/removed/changed nodes, edges, durations, and costs.

tools.name: osop.optimize
tools.description: Synthesize an optimized .osop workflow from multiple .osoplog execution records. Analyzes patterns, bottlenecks, and failure modes to produce a better workflow.

base_osop_path.description: Optional base .osop file to optimize (if omitted, generates from scratch).

prompt_only.description: Output the optimization prompt without calling LLM.

tools.name: osop.view
tools.description: Render a .sop file into standalone HTML. Reads all referenced .osop workflows and produces a self-contained document you can open in any browser.

output_path.description: Where to write the HTML (default: <sop-id>.html next to the .sop).

lang.description: UI language for the rendered HTML.
